opp.name: LC Waikiki S2C Nov 2025
opp.next_step: Nov 18, 2025 Menachem and Nishita conducted a discovery session with Waikiki procurement team Caroline had met the procurement director at DPW recently and they had demonstrated interest in Zycus Agentic AI procurement solution During the discovery call we learned about their current processes and what they are looking to achieve and improve their procurement management Menachem proposed an in person meeting in Istanbul for the week of Dec. 8. They will coordinate internally and provide options Menachem also shared the discovery slides with the prospect. Demo meeting to be scheduled onsite around 8th December
opp.ais_why:
• Nov 2025 Avoma notes capture LC Waikiki's procurement team explicitly stating they have been 'leading AI and ERP process development projects for ~5 years' with the Merlin Intake assistant flagged as their primary interest alongside the supplier performance module. The 5-year self-described AI track record is a verbatim buyer-side credential ‚Äî this is not a green-field AI evaluation but an established AI-led procurement organisation evaluating Zycus's intake assistant specifically.
• Heavy AI product mix in scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake, AppXtend) at $275K Formal Evaluation stage. 4 Avoma meetings Nov 2025 - Jan 2026 including an 18-person customer team on the 14-Jan call, indicating broad stakeholder engagement. Structured assessment underway. The product-scope breadth plus the buyer's self-identified AI maturity creates strong alignment.
• High confidence: Avoma notes directly document AI/Merlin Intake interest with a verbatim 5-year AI-experience claim from the customer-side team. BD_Engagement is marked Unengaged in SFDC which tempers the score (data-quality gap likely). Held at 7 (not 8-9) because no explicit POC commitment or RFP-mandate yet; a confirmed POC scope would push to 8.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz (Indirect Procurement Strategy and Process Development Group Manager)
Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ozan Hazir (Logistics Procurement Director)
Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Menachem (Zycus) on tail-spend math
metrics.narrative: Business problem confirmed: LC Waikiki seeks to modernize indirect procurement operations across nine categories and 70 staff globally, managing supplier performance and tail-spend inefficiencies. Ufuk Sensoz stated on 18 November 2025 that the procurement team has been 'working on AI related process development projects and also ERP develop ERP based IT projects' for approximately 5 years and completed a 'PWC workshop and set up internal procurement initiatives with 2-, 6-, and 12-month project timelines.' Pain points identified: existing Turkish RFQ/e-auction tool experienced 'event downtime' (Ozan Hazir, 18 November 2025), and contract e-signature tool 'Paperzero lacks efficiency and esignature approval' (Ufuk, 18 November 2025). Tail spend represents approximately 20% of total spend, flagged by Menachem as 'significant savings potential.' However, NO quantified savings target, cost reduction goal, cycle-time improvement, or ROI requirement was stated by the buyer. The pain is qualitative (downtime, inefficiency, manual processes) but not yet translated into a defensible business case with numbers. Gap: no stated financial impact, no confirmed procurement budget, no quantified value case the Economic Buyer or CFO has approved. Making the pain quantifiable and tying it to a board-level initiative is the critical next step.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir
Avoma notes: 'Discuss internally with IT and procurement leaders and plan a follow-up demo' (action item, 18 November 2025)
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: 15 contacts, none marked 'Champion'; Ufuk and Ozan marked 'Decision Maker' (operational roles, not commercial authority)
Evidence of passive engagement: no introductions, no follow-through on scheduled demos, 147 days dark since 30 January 2026
champion.narrative: No champion identified. The 18 November 2025 discovery call included two engaged procurement directors—Ufuk Sensoz (Indirect Procurement Strategy and Process Development Group Manager) and Ozan Hazir (Logistics Procurement Director)—both of whom demonstrated interest in Zycus AI capabilities and modular approach. Ufuk stated the team is 'very much into the topic' of AI procurement and flagged supplier performance management and Merlin Intake as 'primary interests.' Ozan confirmed the CEO views AI procurement as a 'hot topic' and expressed willingness to host an on-site demo ('Face to face presentation getting entirely, you know, much more effective'). However, neither has exhibited champion behavior: no internal advocacy, no introduction to the Economic Buyer or IT leadership, no sharing of internal timelines or budget, no pushing back against competitors or internal build options, and no driving the agenda forward post-discovery. The relationship is passive and consultative, not active or politically aligned. Both Ufuk and Ozan deferred next steps to 'discuss internally' and 'invite couple of more colleagues,' and no follow-up occurred despite three scheduled touchpoints (December 2025 on-site demo, January 2026 GenAI event, January 2026 solution demo). The deal has no internal advocate with access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker who is actively selling Zycus inside LC Waikiki. Without a champion, the deal will remain stuck in consensus-building limbo. Developing Ufuk or Ozan into a champion requires: (1) quantifying the pain they own into a business case they can present to the CEO/CFO, (2) positioning them as the AI procurement transformation leader internally, and (3) securing their commitment to introduce Zycus to the Economic Buyer and IT leadership with a clear ask and timeline.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ozan Hazir and Ufuk Sensoz
Avoma notes: 'Existing RFQ and e-auction processes managed via Turkish tool, experienced event downtime'
Avoma notes: 'Procurement team leading AI and ERP process development projects for ~5 years'
Salesforce Competitors__c field: null
competition.narrative: No external competitor named or confirmed. On 18 November 2025, Ozan Hazir stated LC Waikiki is 'actively using procurement tools for RFQ and e auction processes' from a Turkish vendor (company name mentioned as present at the DPW event where Caroline met the buyer, but not transcribed clearly). This incumbent Turkish tool is the 'do nothing' / status quo alternative and is the primary competitive threat, not a named SaaS peer. The buyer did not mention evaluating Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, or any other enterprise S2P platform. However, a significant internal competitive threat exists: LC Waikiki operates an in-house IT development team of approximately 1,000 people (per Ozan, 18 November 2025), and Ufuk stated the team is 'working on AI related process development projects and also ERP develop ERP based IT projects' for 5 years, signaling a build-versus-buy dynamic. The IT organization may view procurement transformation as an internal development opportunity rather than a buy decision, which is consistent with Ufuk's comment 'we need to understand what is our IT management in terms of all those solutions' and the requirement to involve IT leaders before proceeding. The true competitive wedge is demonstrating that Zycus' pre-built, AI-native, modular platform delivers faster time-to-value (5–6 months vs. multi-year internal build), lower total cost of ownership, and enterprise-grade capabilities (116-language support, SAP/Dynamics integration, Gartner-recognized) that an in-house team cannot match. The deal stalled because the buyer has not yet made a build-versus-buy decision, and no external procurement platform RFP or competitive shortlist has been initiated. Without a formal evaluation forcing function, the status quo (incumbent tool + internal build) will win by default.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ozan Hazir (Logistics Procurement Director)
Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz (Indirect Procurement Strategy and Process Development Group Manager)
Avoma notes: 'Existing RFQ and e-auction processes managed via Turkish tool, experienced event downtime'
Avoma notes: 'Local eSignature via Paperzero lacks efficiency and esignature approval, precluding Docusign integration'
Avoma notes: 'Tail spend constitutes ~20% of total spend, offering significant savings potential'
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and multi-dimensional, articulated by two procurement directors on 18 November 2025. Operational pain: (1) existing Turkish RFQ/e-auction tool experiences 'event downtime' during live sourcing events, disrupting procurement operations (Ozan Hazir); (2) contract e-signature tool (Paperzero) 'lacks efficiency and esignature approval' and requires 'train business partners and integrat eSignature processes,' hindering adoption (Ufuk Sensoz); (3) manual, fragmented procurement processes across nine indirect categories and 70 global staff, with no unified source-to-pay platform. Strategic pain: (4) tail-spend management is reactive and unoptimized, representing approximately 20% of total spend with 'significant savings potential' unrealized (Menachem, citing industry standard); (5) supplier risk and performance monitoring is manual or non-existent, flagged by Ufuk as a 'primary interest' area. Organizational pain: (6) the procurement team is running AI and ERP development projects in-house ('approximately thousand people in the IT company,' per Ozan) and has invested in a PWC workshop and multi-phase procurement initiatives, but progress is slow and IT-dependent ('we need to understand what is our IT management,' Ufuk). The pain owner is Ufuk Sensoz (Indirect Procurement Strategy and Process Development Group Manager), who is leading the AI/process transformation agenda, supported by Ozan Hazir (Logistics Procurement Director) and the broader 70-person indirect procurement organization. Pain is real and recent (stated November 2025), but not yet quantified into a financial or time-based business case that would justify $275k investment to a CFO.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir
Avoma notes: 'Local eSignature via Paperzero lacks efficiency and esignature approval, precluding Docusign integration'
Avoma notes: 'Typical implementation spans 5–6 months, with ERP integration phase of 2–3 weeks'
paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics are unknown. No MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement approval workflow has been discussed or documented. The 18 November 2025 discovery call surfaced that LC Waikiki uses a local Turkish e-signature platform (Paperzero) that 'lacks efficiency' and is not integrated with Docusign, and Ufuk stated 'we are not integrated this Docusign solution because this is not approved by the government,' indicating regulatory or compliance constraints on contracting tools in Turkey. Ozan asked whether Zycus contract management could support 'negotiation with your supplier' and manage a 'contract database,' but no Zycus contracting module was scoped, and the buyer stated they already have a Microsoft-based contract database, so contract management was deprioritized. No partner (SI, reseller, or implementation partner) has been mentioned. Implementation timeline was estimated at 5–6 months with a 2–3 week ERP integration phase (per Nishita, 18 November 2025), but no formal SOW, project plan, or commercials have been proposed. The deal has not reached the paper-process stage; without Economic Buyer engagement and a formal RFP/BRD, contracting is premature. First step: map the procurement and legal approval chain, understand local e-signature and data residency requirements, and confirm whether a Turkish partner or reseller is required.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir statements
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query: no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role assigned
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not identified. No contact role carries the 'Economic Buyer' or 'Executive Sponsor' designation. The 18 November 2025 discovery call included two mid-level procurement directors (Ozan Hazir, Logistics Procurement Director; Ufuk Sensoz, Indirect Procurement Strategy Group Manager), both operational leads, neither with budget authority at the enterprise level for a $275k SaaS investment. Ufuk stated they must 'discuss internally with IT and procurement leaders' and noted 'there is another certain timeline' driven by IT management's approach, signaling that approval authority sits above the call participants. Ozan mentioned the topic is 'hot' for 'not just procurement, also for the CEO,' but no C-level executive (CFO, CPO, CEO, CIO) has been named, introduced, or engaged. The deal is structured as a bottoms-up evaluation by functional leads who cannot commercially commit. The Economic Buyer—likely the CFO or a C-level procurement/IT executive who controls the enterprise software budget—has never appeared. Without Economic Buyer engagement, pricing, ROI approval, and contract negotiation cannot proceed. This is a critical gap and the primary reason the deal has stalled.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir
Salesforce OpportunityContactRole: Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir marked as Decision Maker (operational roles, not commercial authority)
Avoma notes: 'Plan to involve IT and procurement leaders in targeted module demos for management alignment'
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is bottoms-up and consensus-driven, led by mid-level procurement and IT stakeholders, with no executive escalation visible. The 18 November 2025 discovery call included two procurement directors (Ozan Hazir, Ufuk Sensoz) who stated they must 'discuss internally with IT and procurement leaders' and 'invite couple of more colleagues from IT department and procurement leaders' for follow-up demos. Ufuk noted 'we need to understand what is our IT management in terms of all those solutions' and referenced 'another certain timeline' set by IT, signaling that IT architecture and the internal development team (approximately 1,000 IT staff, per Ozan) hold veto power. The approval chain is multi-layered: procurement functional leads → IT leadership → senior procurement/C-level (CFO or CEO, mentioned but not engaged). No single Decision Maker has been named; two contact roles are marked 'Decision Maker' (Ufuk Sensoz, Ozan Hazir) but both are operational, not commercial. The process appears to be: (1) discovery and module selection by procurement, (2) IT feasibility and integration review, (3) budget and commercial approval by C-level (not yet reached). The deal stalled after step 1 (discovery) and never advanced to step 2 (IT deep-dive) or step 3 (executive buy-in), despite three scheduled follow-up touchpoints in December 2025 and January 2026 that show no recorded engagement.

sources: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir
Avoma notes: 'Modular Zycus solution allows selection of desired areas; supplier performance management and Merlin Intake assistant were flagged as primary interests'
Avoma notes: 'API-based integration strategy leverages pre-built SAP S/4HANA connectors and Dynamics APIs, requiring 2–3 weeks for integration within a 5–6 month rollout'
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria partially confirmed from the 18 November 2025 discovery call. Ufuk Sensoz stated primary interest in 'supplier risk and performance monitoring module' and 'Merlin Intake assistant,' noting the team had completed a PWC workshop and established 2-, 6-, and 12-month procurement project timelines. Ozan Hazir highlighted the need for a solution that addresses 'contract management' challenges (current Paperzero e-signature tool is inefficient) and sourcing/RFQ downtime issues. The buyer confirmed dual-ERP environment (Microsoft Dynamics 365 for indirect categories, SAP S/4 HANA for production/retail) and asked specifically about integration timelines (Ozan: 'How long does it take for the integration process?'), API-based architecture, and modular flexibility ('Can we just pick up one of the solution, or is it your sales strategies?'). Language support for Turkish, Russian, and Arabic was flagged as essential due to global procurement team distribution. However, NO formal RFP, no weighted scorecard, no documented Business Requirements Document (BRD), and no stated competitive evaluation framework have been shared. The criteria remain implicit and conversational, not structured or ranked. Without a formal RFP or BRD, the buyer has no forcing mechanism to compare vendors or finalize a decision, which is consistent with the 147-day stall.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Hungry. LC Waikiki is actively investing in AI procurement transformation: Ufuk Sensoz stated the procurement team has been 'working on AI related process development projects... for 5 years' and is 'very much into the topic.' The CEO views AI procurement as a 'hot topic' (per Ozan Hazir). The buyer flagged Merlin Intake and supplier performance AI as 'primary interests' and responded enthusiastically to the agentic AI demo. LC Waikiki attended the DPW event specifically to explore AI procurement solutions (where Caroline met them). The organization has invested in a PWC procurement workshop and launched 2-, 6-, and 12-month AI-driven procurement initiatives. AIS fields not populated in Salesforce, but Avoma discovery call confirms strong AI appetite and strategic alignment. The AI fit is real and buyer-initiated; the deal stalled not because of AI misalignment but because the AI value proposition was never quantified or elevated to the Economic Buyer and IT leadership who control AI investment budgets.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 19 November 2025 following Caroline's meeting with LC Waikiki procurement director at DPW event. Discovery call conducted 18 November 2025 with Ozan Hazir and Ufuk Sensoz; Zycus presented full S2P suite, Merlin AI, and modular approach; buyer expressed interest in supplier performance management and Merlin Intake; on-site demo proposed for week of 8 December 2025. Three subsequent touchpoints scheduled (11 December 2025 solution demo, 13 January 2026 EY GenAI event, 14 January 2026 solution demo) but no recorded engagement content. Last Salesforce activity 30 January 2026. Deal has been dark for 147 days with no buyer contact, no field updates, no tasks logged, and no documented follow-through on scheduled demos. Next Step field last updated 1 June 2026 still references November 2025 discovery and December 2025 demo plan, confirming no forward movement.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025; Salesforce activity history (147 days dark); OpportunityContactRole analysis
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir are engaged and expressed interest in Zycus AI capabilities during 18 November 2025 discovery call, but both are passive and have exhibited no champion behaviors (no internal advocacy, no Economic Buyer introduction, no agenda ownership, no follow-through across 147 days and three scheduled touchpoints). The relationship is consultative and bottoms-up, stuck at the operational procurement layer with no path to C-level or IT leadership. Without a champion who can sell Zycus internally and broker access to the Economic Buyer and IT decision-makers, the deal will remain dark indefinitely. Developing a champion requires re-engaging Ufuk or Ozan with a quantified ROI case they can present to the CEO/CFO, positioning them as the AI procurement transformation leader, and securing their commitment to multi-thread upward.

items.action: Re-engage Ufuk Sensoz or Ozan Hazir directly to diagnose the 147-day silence and confirm whether LC Waikiki is still evaluating procurement platforms.

items.expected_effect: Addresses IT-gating blocker; demonstrates pre-built SAP HANA and Dynamics 365 integration vs. multi-year internal build; neutralizes build-versus-buy threat by proving faster time-to-value and lower TCO.

items.expected_effect: Provides operational champions (Ufuk, Ozan) with CFO-ready business case to present upward; quantifies urgency and cost of inaction; converts qualitative pain into board-level AI transformation narrative.

items.action: Offer phased pilot starting with single high-value module (supplier performance or Merlin Intake) to de-risk decision and break consensus gridlock.

items.expected_effect: Creates low-risk entry point bypassing full S2P evaluation; demonstrates Merlin AI value in production within 60–90 days; builds champion advocacy and executive proof-of-value for phase 2 expansion.

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals; deal is cold and relationship has deteriorated. Last buyer engagement was 30 January 2026 (147 days ago). Three scheduled follow-up touchpoints (11 December 2025 demo, 13 January 2026 GenAI event, 14 January 2026 solution demo) show completed/scheduled status but no recorded content, no documented buyer participation, no evidence of forward movement. Buyer committed to 'discuss internally' and 'invite IT and procurement leaders' to follow-up demos (18 November 2025) but never reconvened. No tasks logged, no contact role updates, no Salesforce field changes since January 2026. Next Step field last updated 1 June 2026 still references November 2025 discovery and December 2025 demo plan, confirming no new information. The relationship is not cooling; it is frozen. Re-engagement probability is low without new executive-level introduction or forcing event.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 218 (close date 30 January 2027). Created 19 November 2025; 219 days old. Last activity 30 January 2026, now 147 days ago. Stage: Formal Evaluation since creation (219 days in stage). No verified buyer engagement for 147 days meets the Off Track threshold (60+ days cold). Close date of January 2027 is 7 months out but deal has been dark through the entire H1 2026 period when multi-threading, Economic Buyer mapping, and use-case validation should have been underway.
evidence: Last Salesforce activity: 30 January 2026 (147 days ago)

Four Avoma touchpoints discovered: 18 November 2025 discovery call (transcribed), 11 December 2025 demo (completed, no recording), 13 January 2026 EY GenAI event (not recorded), 14 January 2026 solution demo (completed, no recording)
Next Step field (last updated 1 June 2026) references November 2025 discovery and proposes December 2025 on-site demo, but three subsequent scheduled meetings show no engagement content
No contact role activity, no tasks logged, no field movement since January 2026
Pipeline forecast category with zero recent momentum is indefensible; deal should be Omitted or disqualified pending re-engagement
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains frozen for 147 days with zero buyer engagement since January 2026. No multi-threading, no Economic Buyer mapping, and no forcing event to overcome status quo inertia against strong internal IT build preference.

flags: Economic Buyer not identified: No CFO, CPO, CEO, or C-level budget authority engaged. Discovery participants (Ozan Hazir, Ufuk Sensoz) are operational procurement directors without commercial approval power for $275k investment. Deal cannot progress to pricing or contracting without Economic Buyer sponsorship. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025; SF contact roles)

No champion with Economic Buyer access: Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir are engaged but passive; no internal advocacy, no EB introduction, no agenda ownership. Relationship consultative, not politically aligned. Both deferred next steps to 'discuss internally' with no follow-through across 147 days and three scheduled touchpoints. Deal has no internal advocate selling Zycus upward to C-level or IT. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025; SF activity history)

Single-thread risk (operational layer only, no executive or IT multi-threading): All engagement limited to two mid-level procurement directors; no C-level, no IT, no finance, no legal stakeholders involved. Deal structurally stuck at operational consensus layer with no escalation path. Must multi-thread to Economic Buyer and IT leadership or deal will remain dark. (Source: SF contact roles; Avoma manifest)
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is cold and structurally blocked at the operational procurement layer with no path to Economic Buyer or IT leadership. Three critical gaps: (1) no Economic Buyer identified or engaged (CFO/CPO/CEO level budget authority missing), (2) no IT leadership involvement despite IT-gating requirement and 1,000-person in-house development team posing build-versus-buy threat, (3) no champion with internal advocacy or EB access. Deal stalled 147 days ago after discovery call because buyer deferred to 'discuss internally' and never reconvened despite three scheduled follow-ups. Without executive escalation and a quantified ROI case, this remains a bottoms-up evaluation that cannot convert. Re-engagement must start with Economic Buyer mapping and IT leader engagement, not chasing stale operational-level demos.

competitive_position.summary: No external named competitor. Primary competitive threat is the status quo: LC Waikiki's incumbent Turkish RFQ/e-auction tool (experiencing downtime but operational) plus the internal IT build option (1,000-person IT team actively working on AI and ERP projects for 5 years). The buyer has not initiated a formal RFP or competitive evaluation of enterprise S2P platforms. The deal stalled because LC Waikiki has not made a build-versus-buy decision, and the IT organization's involvement is a gating requirement ('we need to understand what is our IT management in terms of all those solutions,' Ufuk Sensoz, 18 November 2025). Without a forcing event (executive mandate, failed internal build, regulatory or audit pressure), the incumbent plus internal development will win by inertia. The wedge: position Zycus as the fast-path alternative (5–6 months to value vs. multi-year build), pre-integrated with SAP HANA and Dynamics 365, with enterprise-grade AI (Merlin) and 116-language global support that the internal team cannot deliver on their own timeline.

competitors.how_we_win: Reliability and uptime advantage: position Zycus as enterprise-grade alternative with 99.9% SLA, no downtime risk, and cloud-native architecture vs. incumbent's operational failures.

competitors.name: Internal IT build (in-house development)
competitors.quote: We have our own developers, approximately thousand people in the IT company... we need to understand what is our IT management in terms of all those solutions. Procurement team leading AI and ERP process development projects for ~5 years.
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ozan Hazir and Ufuk Sensoz
competitors.how_we_win: Time-to-value wedge: demonstrate that Zycus delivers production-ready, Gartner-recognized S2P + AI in 5–6 months vs. multi-year internal build cycle, with lower TCO, continuous innovation, and zero R&D risk. Bring a TCO model and reference the IT team's 5-year AI project timeline as proof that build is too slow for the CEO's 'hot topic' urgency.

competitors.name: Do nothing (status quo)
competitors.quote: No formal RFP, no competitive shortlist, 147 days dark with no documented progress despite three scheduled follow-up touchpoints.
competitors.source: Salesforce activity history and Avoma manifest
competitors.how_we_win: Create urgency: quantify the cost of inaction (downtime losses, tail-spend leakage, manual inefficiency, delayed AI transformation) and tie it to the CEO's stated priority. Position re-engagement as a low-risk pilot or phased rollout (single module: supplier performance or Merlin Intake) to break the consensus gridlock.

ai_positioning_strength.score: Strong at operational layer; under-positioned at executive layer
ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning was strong and well-received during the 18 November 2025 discovery call. Zycus led with Merlin AI and agentic intake as the centerpiece narrative, and both Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir responded positively. Ufuk stated the team is 'working on AI related process development projects... for 5 years' and is 'very much into the topic,' and flagged Merlin Intake as a 'primary interest.' Menachem positioned Merlin as differentiated ('built from the bottom up, embedded into the system as opposed to bolt-on') and demonstrated intake assistant live from Microsoft Teams, which resonated with the buyer's adoption and change-management concerns. However, the AI narrative was not translated into a quantified business case or executive-level value proposition (no ROI model, no CFO pitch, no CEO-level AI transformation story), so it remained aspirational at the operational layer and never escalated to decision-makers. The AI positioning is sound but under-activated: it won operational interest but did not create executive urgency or unlock budget. To strengthen: tie Merlin's tail-spend negotiation agent (ANA) and supplier risk AI to quantified savings and risk-mitigation outcomes, and position Zycus as the fast-path AI procurement transformation platform vs. the 5-year internal build timeline.

items.quote: Highlighted key interest in supplier risk and performance monitoring module

items.criterion: Dual-ERP integration (Microsoft Dynamics 365 + SAP S/4 HANA)

items.quote: It will be not our first choice because we have already that database of Microsoft system, and for negotiation, it is hard to implement also the resistance of internal business partner because it's not just us. You need to also train train the other internal partners, align with load department, integration with the eSignature process if possible, etcetera. Local eSignature via Paperzero lacks efficiency and esignature approval, precluding Docusign integration.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir

items.quote: No quantified savings target, cost reduction goal, or financial ROI stated by buyer during discovery call. Pain articulated qualitatively (downtime, inefficiency, tail-spend opportunity) but not translated into board-level business case.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025 (absence of quantified metrics)
items.criterion: ROI and quantified business case (CFO/CEO level)

items.quote: We need to understand what is our IT management in terms of all those solutions. Therefore, there is another certain timeline. invite couple of more colleagues from IT department and procurement leaders.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 18 November 2025, Ufuk Sensoz
items.criterion: IT architecture review and feasibility (in-house IT team involvement)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong product-market fit confirmed on functional and technical dimensions during 18 November 2025 discovery call. LC Waikiki's stated needs (supplier performance management, AI-driven intake, RFQ/e-auction reliability, dual-ERP integration, multi-language support) align precisely with Zycus' modular S2P suite, Merlin AI, and pre-built SAP HANA / Dynamics 365 connectors. Buyer explicitly validated modular approach ('Can we just pick up one of the solution?'), 5–6 month implementation timeline, and API-based integration strategy. However, fit is unproven at the Economic Buyer and IT leadership level (no engagement, no BRD, no formal evaluation criteria), and the buyer deprioritized Zycus contract management due to existing Microsoft-based solution and local e-signature constraints. Fit gaps: (1) no quantified ROI case aligned to CFO/CEO priorities, (2) no IT architecture review or integration deep-dive conducted, (3) no reference customer in Turkey or similar dual-ERP retail environment shared. The deal stalled not because of product misalignment but because fit was never elevated beyond the operational procurement layer to the executive and IT decision-makers who control budget and architecture.

hard.ais_why:
• Nov 2025 Avoma notes capture LC Waikiki's procurement team explicitly stating they have been 'leading AI and ERP process development projects for ~5 years' with the Merlin Intake assistant flagged as their primary interest alongside the supplier performance module. The 5-year self-described AI track record is a verbatim buyer-side credential ‚Äî this is not a green-field AI evaluation but an established AI-led procurement organisation evaluating Zycus's intake assistant specifically.
• Heavy AI product mix in scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake, AppXtend) at $275K Formal Evaluation stage. 4 Avoma meetings Nov 2025 - Jan 2026 including an 18-person customer team on the 14-Jan call, indicating broad stakeholder engagement. Structured assessment underway. The product-scope breadth plus the buyer's self-identified AI maturity creates strong alignment.
• High confidence: Avoma notes directly document AI/Merlin Intake interest with a verbatim 5-year AI-experience claim from the customer-side team. BD_Engagement is marked Unengaged in SFDC which tempers the score (data-quality gap likely). Held at 7 (not 8-9) because no explicit POC commitment or RFP-mandate yet; a confirmed POC scope would push to 8.
hard.next_step:

  • Menachem and Nishita conducted a discovery session with Waikiki procurement team
  • Caroline had met the procurement director at DPW recently and they had demonstrated interest in Zycus Agentic AI procurement solution
  • During the discovery call we learned about their current processes and what they are looking to achieve and improve their procurement management
  • Menachem proposed an in person meeting in Istanbul for the week of Dec. 8.
  • They will coordinate internally and provide options
  • Menachem also shared the discovery slides with the prospect.

Demo meeting to be scheduled onsite around 8th December

Deal cannot progress to pricing or contracting without Economic Buyer sponsorship. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025; SF contact roles)

value.flag: IT leadership missing from evaluation: Buyer stated IT management approval required and must 'invite IT department colleagues' to demos, but no IT leader (CIO, IT Director, ERP lead) introduced or engaged across four touchpoints. LC Waikiki operates 1,000-person in-house IT team actively building AI/ERP solutions, posing build-versus-buy threat. Without IT architecture review and integration feasibility sign-off, deal is blocked. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025, Ufuk Sensoz)

value.flag: No champion with Economic Buyer access: Ufuk Sensoz and Ozan Hazir are engaged but passive; no internal advocacy, no EB introduction, no agenda ownership. Relationship consultative, not politically aligned. Both deferred next steps to 'discuss internally' with no follow-through across 147 days and three scheduled touchpoints. Deal has no internal advocate selling Zycus upward to C-level or IT. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025; SF activity history)

value.flag: No quantified ROI or CFO-level business case: Pain articulated qualitatively (downtime, inefficiency, tail-spend leakage ~20% of spend) but no financial savings target, cost reduction goal, or ROI model built. Without quantified business case tied to CEO's stated 'hot topic' priority, Economic Buyer cannot justify $275k investment. Build ROI workshop deliverable co-created with Ufuk/Ozan to take to CFO. (Source: Avoma 18 Nov 2025)

value.flag: Single-thread risk (operational layer only, no executive or IT multi-threading): All engagement limited to two mid-level procurement directors; no C-level, no IT, no finance, no legal stakeholders involved. Deal structurally stuck at operational consensus layer with no escalation path. Must multi-thread to Economic Buyer and IT leadership or deal will remain dark. (Source: SF contact roles; Avoma manifest)

gaps: Three scheduled follow-up touchpoints (December 2025, January 2026) discovered but no transcript or notes content available; cannot confirm buyer participation or engagement quality

IT leadership (CIO, IT Director, ERP lead) mentioned as gating requirement but never identified by name or engaged
Economic Buyer (CFO, CPO, C-level) never named or introduced despite $275k deal size and enterprise software purchase
No formal RFP, BRD, or competitive evaluation documentation shared; decision criteria remain implicit
No quantified ROI, savings target, or financial business case documented in any field or call
